segments.text: Page 58, Conversation. Listen and read, then say.
Come in. I am putting some water in the kettle for tea.
Hi, Grandma. You're limping today. Are you okay?
Come inside, Helen. I'm fine, but I'm feeling a little tired today. I'm stiff from sitting down, so that's why I'm walking oddly.
I'm glad I'm helping you today, Grandma. Where do you want me to start?
First, thank you for coming. Now, sit down. Make yourself at home. I'm getting the photo albums for us to look at.
Super. Are you getting Mom's baby book also?
Of course. I know you love looking at your mom's baby pictures.
You like seeing when you were a young mom. In some ways, you look so different, but in other ways you look the same. Do you ever miss being young, Grandma?
Oh, those were wonderful years. But I'm treasuring my life now. I learned a long time ago to treasure each minute of what I have so I don't miss it later.
That's a good way to deal with it. What are you doing on the weekends now, Grandma?
Well, I'm keeping busy, that's for sure. I ride my bike to the orphanage to visit the children. They would love to meet you. Can you come next weekend?
I'm jotting it down right now, Grandma. You're at the top of my list for next weekend
